# Runtime Requirements Checklist: Local Backend Runtime Orchestration

- **Purpose**: Validate the clarity, completeness, and consistency of requirements for replacing Docker Compose with a unified local backend runtime.
- **Created**: 2026-06-04
- **Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all backend services that must be represented by the local runtime traceable to the architecture overview or another authoritative service inventory? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all required infrastructure dependency categories expanded enough to prevent omissions such as databases, brokers, cache, and media storage? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK003 Does the spec define whether replacement of Docker Compose applies only to backend local development and excludes production deployment behavior? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK004 Are documentation requirements defined for startup, stop, restart, and failure-identification workflows? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements defined for preserving existing local gateway and service addresses after the runtime change? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-005]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "single documented local startup flow" specific enough to distinguish one command or launch action from service-by-service instructions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "required local infrastructure dependencies" defined with enough specificity for planning to identify every dependency? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "local runtime workflow" consistently defined for both status visibility and log access requirements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "recent logs" clarified enough to be objectively assessed during planning and acceptance review? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "normal stop and restart workflows" clear enough to distinguish data preservation from reset or cleanup workflows? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the Docker Compose replacement clarification, FR-008, and assumptions all describe the same replacement scope without conflict? [Consistency, Clarifications, Spec §FR-008, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK012 Do data-retention requirements consistently state that new-runtime data survives restarts while existing Docker Compose data is not migrated? [Consistency, Clarifications, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK013 Do the frontend URL preservation requirements align with the assumption that frontend dev servers are out of scope? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-005, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK014 Do the no-business-change requirements align with the success criteria and user stories, avoiding hidden API/event/domain behavior changes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Spec §SC-002, Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Are success criteria measurable without relying on implementation-specific tooling details? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001-SC-006]
- [ ] CHK016 Does SC-004 define an objective enough threshold for identifying failed status from the runtime view? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK017 Do SC-002 and SC-003 define complete coverage targets for both backend services and infrastructure dependencies? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK018 Does SC-006 establish a clear documentation sufficiency outcome rather than repeating the startup requirement? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 Are primary startup, observability, and URL-preservation scenarios independently testable as written? [Coverage, User Stories 1-3]
- [ ] CHK020 Are exception scenarios for dependency failure and service startup failure represented in both user stories and edge cases? [Coverage, User Story 1, User Story 2, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK021 Are recovery expectations after stopping and restarting the runtime specified for local data created by the new runtime? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK022 Is the scenario of pre-existing Docker Compose data explicitly addressed so implementation does not infer migration work? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011, Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Are port-conflict requirements or expected documentation outcomes defined clearly enough for planning? [Gap, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK024 Are slow dependency startup requirements defined enough to cover readiness ordering and developer visibility? [Gap, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are partial-runtime startup expectations defined, including whether they are supported or only documented as a boundary case? [Ambiguity, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK026 Are unhealthy-service requirements tied to observable dependency causes rather than vague failure visibility? [Clarity, Edge Cases, Spec §FR-009]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK027 Are observability requirements complete across status visibility, logs, and dependency health? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK028 Are reliability expectations for restart and local data preservation expressed as requirement outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK029 Are security and privacy impacts intentionally excluded or covered by the no-business-change requirement? [Gap, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK030 Are performance expectations limited appropriately to startup/status identification outcomes rather than unspecified service runtime performance? [Consistency, Spec §SC-004]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK031 Are required local platform tooling assumptions documented without naming implementation-specific tools in the spec? [Assumption, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK032 Are dependencies on existing architecture overview and service inventory explicit enough for the plan to enumerate services and infrastructure? [Dependency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK033 Is the frontend-out-of-scope assumption precise enough to prevent adding frontend dev server orchestration tasks? [Assumption, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK034 Is the production-deployment exclusion clear enough to prevent infrastructure-as-code or cloud rollout tasks from entering the plan? [Assumption, Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK035 Is the phrase "existing local gateway and backend service addresses" anchored to an authoritative source to avoid disagreement during planning? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK036 Is "required dependency" consistently used for infrastructure dependencies, not downstream services or frontend clients? [Terminology, Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK037 Are replacement, preservation, and non-migration requirements free of contradictions across clarifications, requirements, edge cases, and assumptions? [Conflict, Clarifications, Requirements, Edge Cases, Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist validates requirements writing quality only; it is not an implementation test plan.
